notes.excerpt: # Daily Note — YYYY-MM-DD  ## Today's three

notes.title: Decision — TITLE — YYYY-MM-DD
notes.excerpt: # Decision — TITLE — YYYY-MM-DD  ## What was decided

notes.title: TOPIC
notes.excerpt: # TOPIC  ## Summary

notes.excerpt: # Learning — TITLE — YYYY-MM-DD  ## What I learned

notes.title: Meeting — TOPIC — YYYY-MM-DD
notes.excerpt: # Meeting — TOPIC — YYYY-MM-DD  **Attendees:** _names_

notes.excerpt: # Project — NAME  ## Outcome

notes.excerpt: # Weekly Review — Week of YYYY-MM-DD  ## What I said I'd do

notes.title: Vault Conventions
notes.excerpt: # Vault Conventions  Conventions exist so any file can be read, linked, or rewritten without surprises. Follow them strictly.

notes.title: Git & Repo Conventions
notes.excerpt: # Git & Repo Conventions  The vault is a git repository at `github.com/vladutzeloo/own-jarvis`. This file is the operating manual for any session — Jarvis or human — that touches git or GitHub for this repo.

notes.title: Master Index (MOC of MOCs)
notes.excerpt: # Master Index  The single map that points at every other map. When in doubt, start here.

notes.title: Vault Architecture
notes.excerpt: # Vault Architecture  The vault is organized as a layered system, not a flat list of folders. Each layer answers one question Jarvis asks before acting.

notes.title: About Vladimir
notes.excerpt: # About Vladimir  > **Jarvis: read this first.** Every recommendation should reflect this context. If something here is wrong, ask before assuming.

notes.title: Current Context
notes.excerpt: # Current Context  Short-lived facts about Vladimir's current life situation. Unlike [[01_Identity/about]], this file is expected to drift — review monthly.

notes.title: Goals
notes.excerpt: # Goals  Goals at three time horizons. Jarvis uses these to prioritize suggestions and notice when a request connects to a larger arc.

notes.title: Preferences
notes.excerpt: # Preferences  How Jarvis should communicate, format, and deliver work for Vladimir.

notes.title: Values
notes.excerpt: # Values  What Vladimir uses to weigh tradeoffs when goals conflict. These are deliberately fewer than goals — three to five durable principles, not a virtues list.

notes.title: Capabilities — Overview
notes.excerpt: # Capabilities  The set of things Jarvis can actually do, organized by mechanism. Before recommending an approach, check what's on the menu.

notes.title: Agents — Sub-Claudes
notes.excerpt: # Agents  Sub-Claudes Jarvis can dispatch for parallel, isolated, or specialized work. Each one is its own context window — fresh memory, no awareness of the parent conversation.

notes.title: Agent — Claude Code Guide
notes.excerpt: # Agent: Claude Code Guide  Specialized agent for questions about Claude Code (CLI), Claude Agent SDK, and Claude API.

notes.title: Agent — Explore
notes.excerpt: # Agent: Explore  Fast read-only search agent. Locates code, finds files by pattern, greps for symbols.

notes.title: Agent — General Purpose
notes.excerpt: # Agent: General Purpose  The default workhorse — multi-step research, complex searches, broad investigations.

notes.title: Agent — Plan
notes.excerpt: # Agent: Plan  Software architect agent. Designs implementation plans, identifies critical files, considers architectural trade-offs.

notes.title: Connectors — MCP Servers
notes.excerpt: # Connectors  MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that expose third-party services as tools. Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, Asana, Notion, etc.

notes.title: Integrations
notes.excerpt: # Integrations  External APIs Jarvis can call directly from a script, agent, or shell — distinct from [[02_Capabilities/connectors/README|connectors]] (MCP servers wired into Cowork) and [[02_Capabilities/tools/README|tools]] (atomic primit

notes.title: Google Jules API (jules.google)
notes.excerpt: # Google Jules API  Programmatic access to [Jules](https://jules.google/), Google's autonomous coding agent. Jarvis dispatches a coding task (repo + prompt + branch); Jules works on it asynchronously and returns a PR or diff. The right tool

notes.title: NVIDIA API (build.nvidia.com)
notes.excerpt: # NVIDIA API  NVIDIA's hosted inference catalog — a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that fronts the NIM model catalog (Llama, Mixtral, Nemotron, embeddings, vision, ASR, TTS). Used when Jarvis needs an NVIDIA-served model rather than a Cl

notes.title: Jarvis Runtime
notes.excerpt: # Jarvis Runtime  Skills, tools, connectors, and agents describe **what Jarvis can do**. This file describes **the substrate all of that runs on** — which Claude product is hosting the session, which model is answering, which integrations a

notes.title: Skills — Registry
notes.excerpt: # Skills  Skills are pre-packaged best-practice instructions that Jarvis loads on demand. Each skill is a `SKILL.md` file in `~/AppData/Roaming/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions/skills-plugin/.../skills/<name>/`. When triggered, Jarvis reads

notes.title: Skill — consolidate-memory
notes.excerpt: # Skill: consolidate-memory  Reflective pass over memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.

notes.title: Skill — docx (Word documents)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: docx  Create, read, and edit Microsoft Word documents (.docx) with professional formatting.

notes.title: Skill — pdf (PDF Processing)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: pdf  Comprehensive PDF manipulation — extract text/tables, create new PDFs, merge/split, fill forms, OCR scanned PDFs.

notes.title: Skill — pptx (Presentations)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: pptx  Create, read, and edit PowerPoint presentations and slide decks.

notes.title: Skill — schedule (Scheduled Tasks)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: schedule  Create scheduled tasks that run on demand or automatically on an interval.

notes.title: Skill — setup-cowork
notes.excerpt: # Skill: setup-cowork  Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect tools, try a skill.

notes.title: Skill — skill-creator (Build New Skills)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: skill-creator  Create, edit, evaluate, and optimize skills.

notes.title: UI/UX Pro Max — Design Intelligence
notes.excerpt: # UI/UX Pro Max  Project-scoped Claude skill that provides design intelligence for web and mobile UI work — 67 styles, 161 colour palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types with reasoning rules, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types acros

notes.title: Skill — xlsx (Spreadsheets)
notes.excerpt: # Skill: xlsx  Create, read, edit, and clean Excel spreadsheets and CSV/TSV files. Supports formulas, formatting, charts, and data analysis.

notes.title: Tools — Built-in Primitives
notes.excerpt: # Tools  The atomic primitives Jarvis has at all times. Skills compose these; connectors wrap external services; agents run in their own process. Tools are the floor.

notes.title: Tool — Artifacts (Live HTML Views)
notes.excerpt: # Artifacts  Persistent, self-contained HTML pages that pull fresh data from connectors each time they're opened. Saved across sessions.

notes.title: Tool — Claude in Chrome (Browser Automation)
notes.excerpt: # Claude in Chrome  DOM-aware browser automation for any web app without a dedicated MCP. Much faster and more reliable than clicking pixels.

notes.title: Tool — Computer Use
notes.excerpt: # Computer Use  Take screenshots of Vladimir's desktop and control it with mouse, keyboard, and scroll.

notes.title: Tool — File System (Read, Write, Edit)
notes.excerpt: # File System  Three atomic file tools: `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`. They operate on Vladimir's actual disk, mediated by the Cowork harness.

notes.title: Tool — Scheduled Tasks
notes.excerpt: # Scheduled Tasks  Cron-like recurring runs that fire as fresh Cowork sessions. Each scheduled task is a self-contained prompt that runs without Vladimir present.

notes.title: Tool — Shell (Bash)
notes.excerpt: # Shell (Bash)  Runs shell commands in an isolated Ubuntu 22 sandbox with Python, Node, and common CLI tools preinstalled. Allowlisted network access.

notes.title: Tool — Task List (TodoWrite)
notes.excerpt: # Task List  In-session task tracker. Renders as a widget in Cowork — Vladimir sees progress live.

notes.title: Tool — Web Fetch
notes.excerpt: # Web Fetch  Pull a specific URL and parse its content.

notes.title: Tool — Web Search
notes.excerpt: # Web Search  Search the live web for current information.

notes.title: Workflows — Recipe Library
notes.excerpt: # Workflows  Recipes for tasks that get done more than once. Each workflow is a step-by-step playbook with judgment calls noted explicitly. When Vladimir asks for one of these, Jarvis follows the recipe rather than reinventing.

notes.title: Workflow — Draft Email
notes.excerpt: # Draft Email  Write an email in Vladimir's voice. Not for sending — for drafting that he'll review and send himself.

notes.title: Workflow — Summarize Thread
notes.excerpt: # Summarize Thread  Distill a long email or Slack thread into something readable.

notes.title: Workflow — Code Review
notes.excerpt: # Code Review  Review a PR or diff. The goal is to catch real problems, not produce a long comment list.

notes.title: Workflow — Debug Session
notes.excerpt: # Debug Session  Systematic debugging. The goal is to find the root cause, not just make the symptom go away.

notes.title: Workflow — Create a Presentation
notes.excerpt: # Create a Presentation  Slide deck for a meeting, pitch, or review.

notes.title: Workflow — Create a Report
notes.excerpt: # Create a Report  Word document with formal structure — executive summary, sections, conclusions.

notes.title: Workflow — Create a Spreadsheet
notes.excerpt: # Create a Spreadsheet  Data table, financial model, tracker, or analysis output as Excel.

notes.title: Workflow — Daily Review
notes.excerpt: # Daily Review  Morning planning pass. Five to ten minutes.

notes.title: Workflow — Inbox Zero
notes.excerpt: # Inbox Zero  Process [[07_Inbox/README]] until empty. The vault inbox, not email — though the same logic applies.

notes.title: Workflow — Meeting Prep
notes.excerpt: # Meeting Prep  Get ready for a specific meeting. 5-15 minutes depending on stakes.

notes.title: Workflow — Weekly Review
notes.excerpt: # Weekly Review  Friday afternoon retrospective + next-week plan. 30 minutes.

notes.title: Workflow — Deep Research
notes.excerpt: # Deep Research  Multi-source investigation with synthesis. Use when Vladimir needs to understand a topic well enough to make a decision or explain it to someone else.

notes.title: Workflow — Quick Lookup
notes.excerpt: # Quick Lookup  Fast factual answer. One or two queries, direct response, no synthesis ceremony.

notes.title: Projects — Active Work
notes.excerpt: # Projects  Active work with a defined outcome and a deadline (even a soft one). Each project gets its own folder containing notes, drafts, deliverables, and a `_index.md`.

notes.title: Project Archive
notes.excerpt: # Project Archive  Closed projects, kept for reference. Don't delete — past project folders are some of the best reference material the vault has.

notes.title: Project — VMES
notes.excerpt: # Project — VMES  Virtual Manufacturing Execution System. Industrial analytics and real-time production monitoring with an engineering-grade interface. Vladimir owns it and ships it.

notes.title: VMES — CRM (crm.vmes.ro)
notes.excerpt: # VMES — CRM (crm.vmes.ro)  Vladimir's CRM, served at `crm.vmes.ro`. First-party — built and operated alongside VMES.

notes.title: Track 2 — Monitor the Live Site
notes.excerpt: # Track 2 — Monitor the Live Site  Jarvis fetches vmes.ro (and eventually crm.vmes.ro) on a schedule. Captures uptime, response time, meta drift, and SEO posture. Writes to `04_Projects/vmes/site/monitoring-log.md`.

notes.title: Track 3 — Publish Vault Content to vmes.ro
notes.excerpt: # Track 3 — Publish Vault Content to vmes.ro  Author docs, blog posts, or product pages in the vault. A pipeline pushes them to vmes.ro on commit.

notes.title: Track 4 — Pull Live Data from VMES into Vault
notes.excerpt: # Track 4 — Pull Live Data from VMES into Vault  Expose VMES + CRM data to Jarvis so he can answer questions about production state, customer pipeline, and operational metrics — without Vladimir having to re-explain every time.

notes.title: Track 5 — GitHub Repo Wiring
notes.excerpt: # Track 5 — GitHub Repo Wiring  Give Jarvis structured access to the VMES GitHub repo. Read PRs, issues, releases, commits. Eventually draft PRs and comment.

notes.title: VMES — Integration Roadmap
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Integration Roadmap  Five tracks for linking the vault to VMES. Sequenced by dependency.

notes.title: VMES — Marketing
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Marketing  The demand side of VMES: getting the right people to vmes.ro, converting them to demos, and turning those into customers in the CRM. This folder holds anything Jarvis should remember or run on a recurring basis to suppor

notes.title: VMES — Paid Acquisition
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Paid Acquisition  How VMES spends money to get qualified attention. This file holds the strategy and the per-channel plans; campaign-specific notes (creatives, copy variants, A/B results) go in dated subfiles when active.

notes.title: VMES — Web Scraping
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Web Scraping  What Jarvis fetches from third-party sites on behalf of VMES, why, and where the results land. Distinct from [[04_Projects/vmes/integrations/02-monitor-live-site]], which monitors *Vladimir's own* surfaces — this file

notes.title: VMES — SEO
notes.excerpt: # VMES — SEO  Organic search posture for vmes.ro. Where the site is strong, where it's leaking visibility, and the actions that move ranking-relevant signals.

notes.title: VMES — Source Repo
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Source Repo  GitHub repo backing vmes.ro and crm.vmes.ro. Vladimir owns and ships from it.

notes.title: VMES — Marketing Site (vmes.ro)
notes.excerpt: # VMES — Marketing Site (vmes.ro)  The public face of VMES. Currently a single-page React/Vite SPA.

notes.title: Knowledge — Durable References
notes.excerpt: # Knowledge  Durable subject knowledge. Things that are true regardless of any specific project.

notes.title: Knowledge Domains
notes.excerpt: # Domains  One folder per subject area. Create a domain folder when knowledge in that area starts overflowing into ad-hoc files.

notes.title: Glossary
notes.excerpt: # Glossary  Cross-domain terms. Terms specific to one domain belong in that domain's folder.

notes.title: References
notes.excerpt: # References  External resources worth remembering — books, papers, tools, websites.

notes.title: Memory — Decisions, Learnings, Episodes
notes.excerpt: # Memory  What happened. Append-only. The vault's autobiography.

notes.title: Decisions
notes.excerpt: # Decisions  Notable choices and their reasoning. Use [[99_Templates/decision]] as the template.

notes.title: Episodes
notes.excerpt: # Episodes  What happened on a given day or in a notable session. Daily notes, weekly reviews, meeting notes, post-mortems.

notes.title: Durable Facts
notes.excerpt: # Durable Facts  Small set of facts Jarvis should keep loaded. Update when they change. Unlike most of `06_Memory`, this file is editable in place — it's a snapshot, not a journal.

notes.title: Learnings
notes.excerpt: # Learnings  Things discovered that should outlive the moment. Use [[99_Templates/learning]] as the template.

notes.title: Inbox — Capture Buffer
notes.excerpt: # Inbox  Unprocessed capture. Drop things here when you don't want to file them yet.

notes.title: Templates
notes.excerpt: # Templates  Reusable starting points. Copy a template into the right folder, rename, fill in.

notes.title: Jarvis Knowledge Vault
notes.excerpt: # Jarvis Knowledge Vault  This vault is the long-term memory and operating manual for **Jarvis** — the AI assistant working alongside Vladimir. Every folder serves a specific purpose; nothing is decorative.

notes.title: Welcome
notes.excerpt: # Welcome  This is the **Jarvis Knowledge Vault**. Start at [[README]].
